name: compound-and North Widget units over 120 across the year
prompt: For the North region only, looking at Widget specifically, show the month-by-month trend of units sold but keep only the months where units topped 120.

name: compound-and West Gadget revenue above 2000 by month
prompt: Restrict to Gadget in the West region and only months whose revenue exceeded 2000, then chart revenue for each remaining month.

name: compound-and East Gizmo revenue over 3000 monthly
prompt: Within the East region, for Gizmo only, plot the units across the months but drop any month where Gizmo revenue did not clear 3000.

name: compound-and South Widget under 100 units months
prompt: Just the South region and just Widget, and only the months where fewer than 100 units moved: compare those months' units.

name: compound-and West Gizmo above 30 units trend
prompt: Show how West-region Gizmo units change month to month, excluding any month that sold 30 or fewer units.

name: compound-and Gadget summer regions over 80 units share
prompt: Across June, July and August, for Gadget only, and counting only region-months that hit at least 80 units, break down the share of units by region.

name: compound-and Widget late-spring regions revenue over 2000
prompt: Compare Widget revenue by region for the months May through August, keeping only the region-months whose revenue was above 2000.

name: compound-and North products above 100 units split by product
prompt: For the North region only, draw a separate line per product showing units over the months, but only include months in which that product exceeded 100 units.

name: compound-and Organic Monday Q1 visits over 900
prompt: Looking only at Organic traffic on Mondays during the first quarter, and only days with more than 900 visits, plot the daily visits over time.

name: compound-and Social weekday first-half visits over 500
prompt: For the Social channel, restricted to weekdays in the first half of the year and to days exceeding 500 visits, show the visit trend day by day.

name: compound-and Paid summer weekends visits over 900 impossible
prompt: Show Paid-channel visits over the summer months (June through August) on weekends only, restricted to days with more than 900 visits.

name: compound-and Email Q4 Tuesday visits over 400 boundary
prompt: For Email traffic on Tuesdays in the fourth quarter, keeping only days above 400 visits, chart the visits per day.

name: aggregate-variants mean units each region not the running total
prompt: Don't sum it — I want the mean units figure for each of the four regions, as a bar chart.

name: aggregate-variants peak single-row revenue per product
prompt: For each product, what's the single biggest revenue figure on record? One bar per product showing that peak value.

name: aggregate-variants lowest units product trap not min over time
prompt: Across all months and regions, what is the smallest units number recorded for each product line? Plot it.

name: aggregate-variants count rows per channel above threshold
prompt: How many daily records did each channel rack up where visits cleared 700? Give me a count per channel.

name: aggregate-variants total vs average wording total signups per channel
prompt: I want the grand total of signups accumulated by each channel over the whole year, not a daily average — bar chart.

name: aggregate-variants average daily visits per channel not summed
prompt: Per channel, give me the average visits on a typical day across 2024 — averaged, definitely not totalled. Bars are fine.

name: aggregate-variants coldest reading per sensor min
prompt: What was the coldest temperature each sensor ever logged over the two weeks? One value per sensor.

name: aggregate-variants hottest single reading per sensor max
prompt: Show the hottest single temperature reading recorded by each sensor — just the peak, no averaging.

name: aggregate-variants count days each channel hit slow visits below floor
prompt: Count, per channel, the number of days visits stayed under 300 — a sluggish-day tally for each channel.

name: aggregate-variants average revenue per product Widget excluded compare
prompt: Leaving Widget out, compare the average revenue per row for the remaining two products.

name: aggregate-variants highest single-day visits per channel one bar each
prompt: For every channel, pull its best-ever single day of visits and chart that one number, one bar per channel.

name: aggregate-variants total revenue per region but split by product
prompt: Total revenue for each region, but break every region's bar out into its three products.

name: derived-ratios conversion rate by channel as a comparison
prompt: Compare the signup conversion rate (signups divided by visits) across the four acquisition channels.

name: derived-ratios revenue per unit by region single series
prompt: Show the average selling price per unit (revenue over units) for each sales region.

name: derived-ratios revenue per unit across months split by region
prompt: Plot revenue per unit month by month, drawing one separate line for each region so the regions can be compared.

name: derived-ratios conversion rate excluding the Email channel
prompt: Conversion rate, computed as signups per visit, for every channel except Email - one bar per remaining channel.

name: derived-ratios conversion rate by day of week
prompt: How does the overall signups-to-visits ratio vary across the days of the week? Show all seven weekdays.

name: derived-ratios humidity to temperature ratio per sensor
prompt: For each sensor, show the ratio of humidity to temperature as a single comparison.

name: derived-ratios revenue per unit comparison as bars by product
prompt: Which product line earns the most revenue per unit sold? Draw it as a bar chart comparing the three products.

name: derived-ratios conversion rate per channel over summer split by channel
prompt: Track signups per visit day by day through the summer (June, July, August), with one line for each channel.

name: derived-ratios Organic conversion rate within a date range
prompt: For the Organic channel only, plot the daily conversion rate (signups / visits) between 2024-03-01 and 2024-03-31.

name: derived-ratios conversion rate trend in the first half single line
prompt: Show the site-wide conversion rate trend, signups divided by visits, for each day in the first half of the year.

name: derived-ratios share of conversion efficiency as a pie
prompt: Break down how each channel's signups-per-visit rate contributes, as a pie chart over the four channels.

name: derived-ratios revenue per unit argmax trap one region
prompt: Highlight the region with the highest revenue, then chart its revenue per unit across the months.

name: nested-time-windows organic visits across the first 10 days of January
prompt: Show daily Organic visits over the first 10 days of January 2024 as a line.

name: nested-time-windows temperature trend in the first week of March
prompt: Plot the temperature trend for the first week of March as a line chart.

name: nested-time-windows paid traffic during the last week of June
prompt: Line of Paid channel visits per day during the last week of June.

name: nested-time-windows weekday-only visits in summer
prompt: Show total visits per day on weekdays only during the summer.

name: nested-time-windows visits in spring excluding weekends
prompt: Daily visits across spring, excluding weekends.

name: nested-time-windows second-half visits with weekends removed
prompt: Plot visits per day over the second half of the year, skipping weekends.

name: nested-time-windows humidity in the third week of February
prompt: Show humidity readings during the third week of February as a line.

name: nested-time-windows first ten days of Q1 signups
prompt: Daily signups for the first ten days of Q1 as a line chart.

name: nested-time-windows mid-quarter organic traffic
prompt: Trend of Organic visits in the middle of the second quarter.

name: nested-time-windows email visits between July 1 and August 15
prompt: Line of Email channel visits per day between 2024-07-01 and 2024-08-15.

name: nested-time-windows visits over the last 30 days
prompt: Show daily visits over the last 30 days as a line.

name: nested-time-windows both sensors in the first 10 days of March
prompt: Plot temperature for each sensor across the first 10 days of March as a line chart.

name: multi-measure units and revenue side-by-side bars by month
prompt: For each month, put units sold and revenue right next to each other as grouped bars so I can compare the two figures.

name: multi-measure dual-axis line units vs revenue over the months
prompt: Plot units and revenue together as two lines across the months, but give each its own y-axis since their scales are wildly different.

name: multi-measure each region units vs revenue split bars
prompt: Break it down by region and show each region's units and revenue as bars so I can see both metrics per region.

name: multi-measure visits and signups dual line full year
prompt: Show daily visits alongside signups for the whole year as lines, each on its own axis because signups are tiny next to visits.

name: multi-measure visits vs signups monthly totals two bars
prompt: Aggregate the data into monthly totals and compare total visits versus total signups as two bars per month.

name: multi-measure each channel visits and signups split monthly
prompt: Per channel and per month, chart both visits and signups so I can compare the two measures for every channel.

name: multi-measure units vs revenue area both filled by month
prompt: Draw units and revenue as two filled area charts over the months to see how both build up.

name: multi-measure revenue and units only the West region by month
prompt: Just for the West region, compare revenue and units across the months as two bars per month.

name: multi-measure temperature vs humidity dual line per day
prompt: Show temperature and humidity for each day as two lines with independent y-axes since they sit in different ranges.

name: multi-measure both sensors temperature vs humidity over days
prompt: For both sensors, plot temperature and humidity over the days so each sensor shows its two readings.

name: multi-measure visits and signups in the first quarter dual line
prompt: During Q1 only, show visits and signups as two lines, each with its own scale.

name: multi-measure units versus revenue compare per product
prompt: Compare units against revenue for each product so every product shows both numbers.

name: pie-edge revenue share East+West only
prompt: I only care about the East and West regions - draw me a pie showing how their combined revenue splits between the two products Gadget and Gizmo... actually just show revenue per product for those two regions.

name: pie-edge count of rows by channel
prompt: As a pie, what fraction of the records belong to each marketing channel? Count rows, don't sum anything.

name: pie-edge share of total revenue by product no pie word
prompt: What share of total revenue does each product account for?

name: pie-edge humidity by sensor with nulls
prompt: Give me a pie of total humidity broken out by sensor.

name: pie-edge revenue split products excluding North
prompt: Excluding the North region entirely, how does revenue break down by product? Pie please.

name: pie-edge visits over 1040 single channel
prompt: Pie chart of visit volume by channel, but only counting days where visits exceeded 1040.

name: pie-edge revenue per unit ratio by region
prompt: Show revenue per unit (revenue divided by units) for each region as a pie.

name: pie-edge channel share weekends only
prompt: On weekends only, what's each channel's share of total visits? Pie it.

name: pie-edge units distribution March only sales
prompt: For March, give me a pie of units sold across the four regions.

name: pie-edge signups share over 100 impossible
prompt: Pie of signups by channel, restricted to days with more than 100 signups.

name: pie-edge revenue by region for top product
prompt: Take the single best-selling product by total revenue, then show a pie of that product's revenue across regions.

name: pie-edge temperature share both sensors first week
prompt: During the first week of March, how is total temperature divided between the two sensors? Pie chart.

name: scatter-edge sales units-vs-revenue relationship all rows
prompt: Is there a relationship between units sold and revenue? Plot each sales record as a point with units on the horizontal axis and revenue on the vertical axis.

name: scatter-edge sales North-region units vs revenue point cloud
prompt: For the North region only, show how revenue relates to units across individual records as a point-per-row scatter.

name: scatter-edge sales Widget revenue-vs-units colored intent single category
prompt: Looking only at the Widget product, scatter revenue against units to see the per-record spread.

name: scatter-edge sales colored by region units vs revenue four series
prompt: Scatter units against revenue for every record, with the dots colored by region so each region is its own series.

name: scatter-edge sales two-region series South and East
prompt: Compare South versus East: plot each record as a dot of units vs revenue, split into one series per region for just those two regions.

name: scatter-edge readings temperature-vs-humidity null-dropping
prompt: Show the correlation between temperature and humidity across all sensor readings, one dot per reading with temperature on the x-axis.

name: scatter-edge readings Sensor-A only humidity vs temperature
prompt: For Sensor A alone, scatter humidity against temperature, one point per daily reading.

name: scatter-edge readings colored by sensor both series
prompt: Plot temperature versus humidity for every reading, drawing a separate colored series for each sensor.

name: scatter-edge readings impossible temperature threshold scatter
prompt: Among readings hotter than 21 degrees, scatter temperature against humidity to inspect the relationship.

name: scatter-edge traffic visits-vs-signups full year null-dropping
prompt: Across the whole year, show the relationship between daily visits and signups as a scatter, one dot per channel-day with visits on the horizontal axis.

name: scatter-edge traffic Organic signups vs visits date window January
prompt: During January, for the Organic channel only, scatter signups against visits with one point per day.

name: scatter-edge traffic exclude Organic visits vs signups three channels
prompt: Excluding the Organic channel, plot every remaining channel-day as a dot of visits against signups, colored per channel.

name: sort-limit-topn top-2 products on x by units (limit -> short xLen)
prompt: top 2 products by total units

name: sort-limit-topn top-3 channels on x by visits (limit -> xLen 3)
prompt: show me the top 3 channels by visits

name: sort-limit-topn top-2 products AS SERIES across months (seriesCount 2, not xLen)
prompt: compare the top 2 products by revenue across all months

name: sort-limit-topn 2 regions over months — series-split trap (seriesCount 2, xLen 12)
prompt: the 2 regions with the most units, broken down by each month

name: sort-limit-topn 3 channels as series, date bucketed to month (seriesCount 3)
prompt: the 3 channels with the most visits per month

name: sort-limit-topn 2 channels as series, quarterly buckets (seriesCount 2, xLen 4)
prompt: the 2 channels with the highest total visits per quarter

name: sort-limit-topn bottom-2 regions by revenue (guardrail does NOT fire)
prompt: bottom 2 regions by total revenue

name: sort-limit-topn rank all four channels, no limit (full set, sorted)
prompt: rank the four channels by total visits, highest first

name: sort-limit-topn 5 worst days for signups (date-x ranking, model-dependent limit)
prompt: the 5 worst days for signups in the traffic data

name: sort-limit-topn sort all products asc by revenue (reorder, full set)
prompt: sort products from lowest to highest total revenue

name: sort-limit-topn single highest-revenue product (argmax-style, limit 1)
prompt: the single highest-revenue product

name: sort-limit-topn which 3 months by revenue (top-N on categorical x, model limit)
prompt: which 3 months had the greatest total revenue

prompt: revenue by product where revenue is 1500 or less

prompt: show revenue by region for revenue under 1500

prompt: humidity by sensor on readings where the temperature is exactly 20

name: boundary-thresholds readings temperature strictly below the 6 floor impossible
prompt: temperature trend for readings colder than 6 degrees

prompt: Show the revenue share across products other than Widget as a pie chart.

name: negation-exclusion temperature trend ignoring Sensor A

name: negation-exclusion units by region excluding East and West
prompt: Total units by region, excluding the East and West regions.

name: negation-exclusion daily visits split by channel but not Organic
prompt: Daily visits broken down by channel, but not Organic.

name: negation-exclusion monthly revenue except Widget and Gadget
prompt: Revenue per month for every product except the Widget and the Gadget lines.

name: negation-exclusion pie visits non-Email channels
prompt: Breakdown of total visits by channel for non-Email sources, as a pie.

name: negation-exclusion humidity by sensor other than Sensor B

name: negation-exclusion compare regions all but North and South
prompt: Compare units across regions, all but the North and South.

name: negation-exclusion weekday-only visits excluding weekends

name: negation-exclusion monthly revenue trend everything but Gizmo
prompt: Monthly revenue trend for everything but Gizmo.

name: negation-exclusion temperature over days all sensors except Sensor A
prompt: Temperature over the days for all sensors except Sensor A.

name: ordinal-superlative top region then product breakdown (sales)
prompt: For the region that generates the most revenue, break the units down by product.

name: ordinal-superlative best-revenue product split by region (sales)
prompt: Take the product with the highest total revenue and show its revenue by region.

name: ordinal-superlative winning region revenue across months (sales)
prompt: Which region brings in the most revenue? Plot that region's revenue across the months.

name: ordinal-superlative argmax month then product mix (sales)
prompt: The month that gets the most revenue overall, then revenue by product for it.

name: ordinal-superlative argmin region least units by product (sales)
prompt: For the region that sells the least units overall, show units by product.

name: ordinal-superlative largest-units product one bar per month (sales)
prompt: The product with the largest total units, with one bar per month.

name: ordinal-superlative top quarter signups by channel (traffic)
prompt: Show the quarter with the most signups, then break signups down by channel.

name: ordinal-superlative busiest weekday visits by channel (traffic)
prompt: The weekday that gets the most visits — show visits by channel for that weekday.

name: ordinal-superlative fewest-signups channel visits over time (traffic)
prompt: Find the channel with the fewest total signups and chart its visits over time.

name: ordinal-superlative top channel daily visits (traffic)
prompt: Which channel makes the most visits? Show its daily visits.

name: ordinal-superlative which sensor runs hotter trap (readings)
prompt: Which sensor runs hotter overall, and how does that sensor's temperature change day by day?

name: ordinal-superlative strongest-month non-matching superlative trap (sales)
prompt: The month that has the strongest total revenue — show that month's revenue split by region.

name: cyclic-grouping median-ish wording weekday traffic email
prompt: For the Email channel, roll up the typical number of visits to each of the seven days of the week.

name: cyclic-grouping signup-rate per weekday derived traffic
prompt: Across the seven weekdays, what is the signup-to-visit rate on a typical day?

name: cyclic-grouping day-of-month readings humidity nulls survive
prompt: Take the humidity numbers and average them by calendar day-of-the-month.

name: cyclic-grouping weekday split per sensor readings temperature
prompt: Compare how each sensor's temperature behaves on the different days of the week, one line per sensor.

name: cyclic-grouping month-of-year split by channel area traffic
prompt: Show visits aggregated into the twelve months of the year, with a separate filled area trend for every channel.

name: cyclic-grouping weekday counts no-measure traffic count
prompt: How many records fall on each weekday? Just count the rows per day of the week.

name: cyclic-grouping feb-only day-of-month leap traffic
prompt: Looking only at February, plot the average daily visits against the day number within the month.

name: cyclic-grouping weekday excluding weekends traffic organic
prompt: For Organic only, break average visits out by weekday but leave out Saturday and Sunday.

name: cyclic-grouping quarter cyclic units sales-trap month-text
prompt: Group total visits into the four quarters of the year and chart them.

name: cyclic-grouping month-of-year Q1-window readings-like traffic paid
prompt: Restricting to the first quarter, show average visits for Paid by month of the year.

name: cyclic-grouping day-of-week dual measure traffic
prompt: On an average weekday, how do visits and signups each look across the days of the week?

name: cyclic-grouping weekday share pie traffic social
prompt: For the Social channel, what share of total visits lands on each day of the week? Use a pie.

name: implicit-charttype watch revenue move through the calendar (trend->line)
prompt: I'd like to watch how revenue moves through the calendar month after month

name: implicit-charttype trace evolution of daily visits across 2024 (evolution->line)
prompt: trace the evolution of daily visits across the whole of 2024

name: implicit-charttype how the four channels stack up on visits (compare->bar)
prompt: how do the four marketing channels stack up against each other on total visits

name: implicit-charttype portion of revenue each product accounts for (share->pie|bar)
prompt: what portion of total revenue does each product account for

name: implicit-charttype connection between warmth and humidity (relationship->scatter)
prompt: is there any connection between how warm it gets and how humid it is

name: implicit-charttype do bigger orders go with bigger revenue (relationship->scatter)
prompt: do bigger order sizes tend to go hand in hand with bigger revenue

name: implicit-charttype how each channel develops over the year (trend+split->line series)
prompt: I want to see how each channel's daily visits develop over the year

name: implicit-charttype the makeup of traffic by channel (distribution->pie|bar)
prompt: give me the makeup of overall traffic broken out by channel

name: implicit-charttype put the two sensors side by side on temperature (compare->bar)
prompt: put the two sensors side by side and compare their typical temperature

name: implicit-charttype how temperature drifts day to day over two weeks (trend->line)
prompt: how does the temperature drift from day to day over the two weeks

name: implicit-charttype how revenue is spread across the regions (share->pie|bar)
prompt: how is total revenue spread across the regions

name: implicit-charttype when visits climb do signups climb too (relationship->scatter)
prompt: when visits climb, do signups climb right along with them
